ALAMO CLUB WILL DANCE.
The Alamo club has issued invit-
atoms for dance to be given
Easter Monday at the Elks' temple.
The arrangements are in the hands
of committee with w. j. Kirwig
as chairman. and about 200 guests
have been hidden for the occasion.
This will be the first Of the after
Lenten series and will undoubtedly
he one of the largest attended af-
fairs Of the season.


= = 4
RECITAL is A SUCCESS.


Miss Lajla Skartvedt. a talented
young pianist and pupil of Arthur
Frazer, gave recital last evening
at the assembly loom of the Spo-
kane college. She was assisted by
Miss Florence Mason as violinist
and the program as a whole was
very entertaining.



manner and shows marked ability
as an artist.


MRS. ROBBINS IMPROVING.
Mrs. c. P. Robbins, who has been
at Portland for some time is re-
ported as improving in health, but
she will not be home for several
weeks. Mr.. Robbins returned from
a visit at that place yesterday.
X. OF c. PLANNING SPLENDID


AFFAIR.".


The Knights of Columbus are
making extensive preparations for
the sixth annual ball to be given by
them Monday, March %. at the Hall
of the Doges. The hall will be dec
Grated for the occasion and every
effort is being made to have the
affair one of the most pretentious
yet given by the lodge. One hun
dred and fifty couples have been
included on the guest list.
WOMAN's CLUB HAVE ENJOY


ABLE AFFAIR.


The members of the parliament
airy law class of the Woman's club
had most enjoyable time yester-
day at a meeting at the library.
Several Of the ladies dressed in
costumes representing the most
prominent ladies of the land and
acted out their characters in
most entertaining manner.
